text: Final a i r d r o p window. c o n n e c t your w a l l e t at hxxps://secure-airdrop[.]xyz and c l a i m 5 ETH before it expires.
rationale: Spaced keywords and defanged URL should not hide a wallet-drainer-style airdrop.

text: C0inbase support escalation: c0nnect your w4llet and send a small ETH deposit so we can remove the account restriction.
rationale: Leetspeak should normalize back to wallet/connect and preserve the support-scam transfer request.

text: Airdrop oficial: conecta tu wallet en hxxps://metamask-bono[.]top para reclamar ETH gratis. Ultima oportunidad, expira ahora.
rationale: Spanish-language wallet-drainer phrasing should still reach public-label candidacy.

text: Congratulations, your wallet won 2 BTC. Claim now through bit.ly/free-btc-verify before the eligibility window closes.
rationale: Shortener plus prize/claim language is a strong reviewer evidence signal.

text: MetaMask security update: verify your wallet at metamask-security[.]top to keep access to your tokens.
rationale: A brand-looking domain outside the official domain list should be surfaced as URL evidence.

text: [Image OCR] Claim your bonus token using the QR code. Wallet connect required. Link is cropped. Source account not visible.
rationale: OCR/source ambiguity should route to review rather than trigger automatic public labeling.

text: Un grupo dice que puedo reclamar un airdrop si conecto mi billetera. No he verificado el enlace. Alguien sabe si es legitimo?
rationale: Spanish help-seeking about an airdrop should route to review without a public label.

text: Fraud research note: scammers often use hxxps://secure-airdrop[.]xyz-style pages. Do not connect your wallet or send crypto to these sites.
rationale: Defanged scam examples in research/warning context should suppress public labels.

text: Alerta de estafa: no envies BTC a personas que prometen ganancias garantizadas y no conectes tu wallet a enlaces de airdrop.
rationale: A Spanish consumer warning names scam features but should not be treated as promotion.

text: Debugging a wallet connection bug in our testnet app at dev-wallet[.]local. The issue is a stale session token, not a user-facing security problem.
rationale: Developer/debugging context should suppress public labeling even when wallet terms appear.

text: Uniswap governance discussed whether past users should receive an airdrop. No claim page is live yet and I am not sharing any wallet link.
rationale: Market/governance commentary should not be treated as an airdrop solicitation.

text: The sender domain matches a legitimate project, but the link redirects to a different website: hxxps://project-update[.]click/claim. I have not verified it.
rationale: Redirect mismatch and unverified claim URL should route to review, not automatic public enforcement.
